Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Represents a single chat session or interaction history between a user and the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

title.description: A descriptive title for the conversation, possibly auto-generated or user-provided.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the conversation was initiated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time a message was added or the conversation was modified.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents an individual message exchanged within a conversation, either from the user or the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

content.description: The textual content of the message.

senderType.description: Indicates who sent the message, e.g., 'user' or 'ai'.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent or received.

File.title: File
File.description: Represents metadata for a file uploaded by the user, potentially associated with a message.

id.description: Unique identifier for the File entity.

messageId.description: Reference to the Message this file is attached to. (Relationship: Message 1:N File)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the file, e.g., 'image/png', 'application/pdf'.

fileSize.description: The size of the file in bytes.

fileUrl.description: The URL or path where the file is stored (e.g., in object storage).

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the file was uploaded.

UserSettings.title: UserSettings
UserSettings.description: Stores user-specific preferences and configuration settings for the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserSettings entity. (Assuming a single settings object per local app instance or implicit user).

theme.description: The preferred visual theme for the application (e.g., 'light', 'dark').

llmModelPreference.description: The user's preferred LLM model to interact with (e.g., 'default', 'fast_model', 'creative_model').

autoSaveChatHistory.description: Boolean indicating whether chat history should be automatically saved.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user settings were last updated.

definition.description: Stores user-specific preferences and configuration settings. This is a singleton document per user, implicitly owned by the 'userId' in the path, therefore does not require an explicit 'ownerId' field for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns these settings.

definition.description: Represents a single chat session between a user and the AI. Documents in this collection include a denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching 'userId' from the path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific conversation.

definition.description: Represents an individual message within a conversation. Documents in this collection include denormalized 'ownerId' (matching 'userId' from the path) and 'conversationId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent conversation and this message.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific message.

definition.description: Stores metadata for a file uploaded by the user, attached to a specific message. Documents in this collection include denormalized 'ownerId' (matching 'userId' from the path), 'conversationId', and 'messageId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent conversation, message, and this file.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent message.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific file.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES to ensure a secure, scalable, and debuggable system for Libre Chat. The primary strategy employed is **Path-Based Ownership** combined with **Denormalization of Authorization Context**.

**Authorization Independence:** All user-specific data (Conversations, Messages, Files, User Settings) is nested directly under a `/users/{userId}` path. This establishes a clear and immediate ownership context, eliminating the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules to ascertain the owner of a document or its parent. For `Conversation`, `Message`, and `File` entities, the `ownerId` field, which corresponds to the `userId` from the path, is denormalized and stored directly within each document. This means that any document can be authorized by simply checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId`, making rules atomic, robust, and easy to debug. The `UserSettings` collection, being a singleton subcollection directly under `/users/{userId}/settings`, relies purely on the path for authorization (`request.auth.uid == userId`) as its ownership is unambiguous.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Policies):**
1.  **Structural Segregation:** All documents within a given user's path share the same security posture (private to that user). There are no mixed public/private data within these collections. This simplifies `list` operations, as a query for `/users/{userId}/conversations` inherently only returns conversations belonging to the authenticated `userId`.
2.  **Explicit Ownership:** By embedding `ownerId` in `Conversation`, `Message`, and `File` documents, and leveraging the path for `UserSettings`, all `list` operations automatically enforce ownership. Rules can simply `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` at the collection level, and the system ensures that `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. If a query attempts to list data for a `userId` that does not match `request.auth.uid`, the request will be denied before any data is returned, upholding the QAPs principle.

**Hierarchical Paths:** The structure leverages nested subcollections (`/users/{userId}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}/files/{fileId}`) to logically group related data and reinforce ownership from the root (`users`) down to the leaf (`files`). This provides an intuitive and efficient way to manage relationships and enforce granular, yet simple, security rules.
